n8n-nodes-base.credentials.whatsapp.phon: Please include the country code without the “+”, ex. 85298765432
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.whatsapp.webh: Paste the URL below to Webhook's Callback URL field in the WhatsApp portal.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.whatsapp.veri: In WhatsApp Business Platform, paste the Token below to Verification Token to verify
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.wechat.webhoo: Paste the URL below to Server Address field in the WeChat portal.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.wechat.app_id: The developer ID is an Official Account identification code, which can call Official Account API with developer's password.
In WeChat portal, please identify “Token” and paste it in the field below.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.wechat.secret: In WeChat portal, please identify “Secret” and paste it in the field below.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.wechat.encodi: In WeChat portal, please identify “EncodingAESKey” and paste it in the field below.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.wechat.user_n: In WeChat portal, please identify “Original ID” and paste it in the field below.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.line.webhookU: Paste the URL below to Webhook URL field in the LINE portal.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.aws.rekogniti: If you use Amazon VPC to host workflow system, you can establish a connection between your VPC and Rekognition using a VPC endpoint. Leave blank to use the default endpoint.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.aws.lambdaEnd: If you use Amazon VPC to host workflow system, you can establish a connection between your VPC and Lambda using a VPC endpoint. Leave blank to use the default endpoint.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.aws.snsEndpoi: If you use Amazon VPC to host workflow system, you can establish a connection between your VPC and SNS using a VPC endpoint. Leave blank to use the default endpoint.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.aws.sesEndpoi: If you use Amazon VPC to host workflow system, you can establish a connection between your VPC and SES using a VPC endpoint. Leave blank to use the default endpoint.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.aws.sqsEndpoi: If you use Amazon VPC to host workflow system, you can establish a connection between your VPC and SQS using a VPC endpoint. Leave blank to use the default endpoint.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.aws.s3Endpoin: If you use Amazon VPC to host workflow system, you can establish a connection between your VPC and S3 using a VPC endpoint. Leave blank to use the default endpoint.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.facebookGraph: (Optional) When the app secret is set the node will verify this signature to validate the integrity and origin of the payload
If the URL you get displayed on Freshdesk is "https://company.freshdesk.com" enter "company"
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.freshserviceA: Domain in the Freshservice org URL. For example, in https://imbrace.freshservice.com, the domain is imbrace
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.freshworksCrm: Domain in the Freshworks CRM org URL. For example, in https://imbrace-org.myfreshworks.com, the domain is imbrace-org.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.googleApi.ema: The Google Service account similar to user-808@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.googleApi.pri: Enter the private key located in the JSON file downloaded from Google Cloud Console
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.googleApi.del: The email address of the user for which the application is requesting delegated access
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.googleCloudNa: Google Cloud Natural Language OAuth2 API
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.googleFirebas: Google Firebase Cloud Firestore OAuth2 API
Google Firebase Realtime Database OAuth2 API
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.googleVertexA: The Google Service account similar to user-808@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
The Google supported region similar to us-central1
The Google service endpoint ID, which is for the google cloud service (e.g. Vertex AI). You can check this by visit https://console.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/endpoints

The Google Service account client ID in the json that downloaded when you create the key for the service account, you can check by visit the link https://console.cloud.google.com/iam-admin/serviceaccounts?project=YOUR_PROJECT_NAME
The project ID similar to master-bulwark-366906
Enter the private key located in the JSON file downloaded from Google Cloud Console
The Google Service account similar to https://www.googleapis.com/robot/v1/metadata/x509/user-808@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
The email address of the user for which the application is requesting delegated access
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.imap.allowUna: Whether to connect even if SSL certificate validation is not possible
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.kafka.usernam: Optional username if authenticated is required
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.kafka.passwor: Optional password if authenticated is required
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.kafka.awsAcce: Amazon Web Services Access Key ID
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.kafka.awsSecr: Amazon Web Services Secret Access Key
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.linkedInOAuth: Whether to request permissions to post as an organization
Standard scopes for posting on behalf of a user or organization. See this resource .
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.mqtt.clean.de: Whether to clean the session after disconnecting from the broker
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.mqtt.clientId: The client ID to use when connecting to the MQTT broker
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.postgres.allo: Whether to connect even if SSL certificate validation is not possible
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.mongoDb.conne:
If provided, the value here will be used as a MongoDB connection string,
						  and the MongoDB credentials will be ignored
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.mongoDb.datab: Note: the database should still be provided even if using an override connection string
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.mySql.connect: The milliseconds before a timeout occurs during the initial connection to the MySQL server
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.oAuth2Api.org: Whether to request permissions to post as an organization
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.oAuth2Api.leg: Whether to use the legacy API
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.oAuth2Api.sub: The subdomain of your work environment
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.oAuth2Api.aut: For some services additional query parameters have to be set which can be defined here
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.salesforceJwt: Consumer Key from Salesforce Connected App

Use the multiline editor. Make sure it is in standard PEM format:
-----BEGIN -----
KEY DATA GOES HERE
-----END -----
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.signl4Api.tea: The team secret is the last part of your SIGNL4 webhook URL
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.sshPrivateKey: Passphase used to create the key, if no passphase was used leave empty
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.telegramApi.a: Chat with the bot father to obtain the access token
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.webflowOAuth2: For some services additional query parameters have to be set which can be defined here
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.wooCommerceAp: Whether credentials should be included in the query. Occasionally, some servers may not parse the Authorization header correctly (if you see a “Consumer key is missing” error when authenticating over SSL, you have a server issue). In this case, you may provide the consumer key/secret as query string parameters instead.
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.zendeskApi.su: The subdomain of your Zendesk work environment
n8n-nodes-base.credentials.zendeskOAuth2: The subdomain of your Zendesk work environment
URL to get authorization code. Replace {SUBDOMAIN_HERE} with your subdomain.
URL to get access token. Replace {SUBDOMAIN_HERE} with your subdomain.
For some services additional query parameters have to be set which can be defined here
